context: Tests whether the agent implements Redis velocity checks using ioredis with the correct per-dimension thresholds, time windows, Redis key naming conventions, and proper TTL/EXPIRE handling to avoid keys that never expire.

checklist.name: Uses ioredis
checklist.description: Imports and uses 'ioredis' (not 'redis', 'node-redis', or another package) for Redis access

checklist.name: IP key naming
checklist.description: Uses the key pattern 'velocity:ip:<ip>' (colon-separated, with 'velocity:ip:' prefix) for IP tracking

checklist.name: Email key naming
checklist.description: Uses the key pattern 'velocity:email:<email>' for email tracking

checklist.name: Card key naming
checklist.description: Uses the key pattern 'velocity:card:<fingerprint>' for card fingerprint tracking

checklist.name: Spend key naming
checklist.description: Uses the key pattern 'velocity:spend:<fingerprint>' for daily spend tracking

checklist.name: IP threshold and window
checklist.description: Blocks when IP order count exceeds 10 within a 1-hour window (3600 seconds)

checklist.name: Email threshold and window
checklist.description: Blocks when email order count exceeds 5 within a 24-hour window (86400 seconds)

checklist.name: Card threshold and window
checklist.description: Blocks when card fingerprint usage exceeds 3 within a 24-hour window

checklist.name: Daily spend limit
checklist.description: Blocks when cumulative spend for a card fingerprint exceeds 50000 cents ($500) within a day

checklist.name: EXPIRE on new keys
checklist.description: Calls EXPIRE (or equivalent TTL method) on a Redis key immediately after it is first created (i.e., when the counter was previously zero or absent), not just when it is incremented

checklist.name: Reason codes returned
checklist.description: Returns distinct reason strings for each type of violation: 'ip_velocity_exceeded', 'email_velocity_exceeded', 'card_velocity_exceeded', 'daily_spend_exceeded'

checklist.name: recordSuccessfulTransaction updates spend
checklist.description: The recordSuccessfulTransaction function increments the spend key and sets a TTL if the key is new (ttl < 0 check or equivalent)
